amf_association_failure.line: [0m[93m[NGAP]   No AMF is associated to the gNB
context: [0m[32m[NR_RRC]   [FRAME 00000][gNB][MOD 00][RNTI 9410] [RAPROC] Logical Channel UL-DCCH, processing NR_RRCSetupComplete from UE (SRB1 Active)
[0m[93m[NGAP]   No AMF is associated to the gNB
[0m[F1AP]   CU Task Received SCTP_DATA_IND for Instance 0

amf_association_failure.pattern: No AMF is associated to the gNB

fix_recommendations: Add detailed logging around error location
Validate network configuration and ensure AMF parameters are correctly set in configuration files like AMF IP, port
Ensure PLMN, TAI, and slices in gNB match those in AMF
Check if the AMF is reachable from the gNB
Recheck and correct AMF IP, port, and gNB configuration files: openairinterface5g/targets/PROJECTS/GENERIC-NR-5GC/CONF/cu_gnb.conf
Add a static route to the AMF machine if it is running on a different network or interface: ip route add <AMF_IP> via <gNB_IP> dev <interface>

comprehensive_rca.summary:
Root Cause Analysis for amf_association_failure in NGAP layer

Specification Context:
This functionality is governed by 3GPP 38.413 - NG-RAN; NG Application Protocol (NGAP). 
Recommended Fixes:
- Recheck and correct AMF IP, port, and gNB configuration files: openairinterface5g/targets/PROJECTS/GENERIC-NR-5GC/CONF/cu_gnb.conf
- Validate network configuration and ensure AMF parameters are correctly set in configuration files like AMF IP, port
- Check if the AMF is reachable from the gNB
- Ensure PLMN, TAI, and slices in gNB match those in AMF
- Add a static route to the AMF machine if it is running on a different network or interface: ip route add <AMF_IP> via <gNB_IP> dev <interface>
- Add detailed logging around error location

Impact Assessment:
- User Experience: medium impact
- System Performance: medium impact
- System Stability: medium impact
- Cascade Effects: This issue may affect the following layers:
  * F1AP: Issues in NGAP can propagate to F1AP layer, affecting its functionality
fix_recommendations: Recheck and correct AMF IP, port, and gNB configuration files: openairinterface5g/targets/PROJECTS/GENERIC-NR-5GC/CONF/cu_gnb.conf
Validate network configuration and ensure AMF parameters are correctly set in configuration files like AMF IP, port
Check if the AMF is reachable from the gNB
Ensure PLMN, TAI, and slices in gNB match those in AMF
Add a static route to the AMF machine if it is running on a different network or interface: ip route add <AMF_IP> via <gNB_IP> dev <interface>
Add detailed logging around error location

cascade_effects.description: Issues in NGAP can propagate to F1AP layer, affecting its functionality

ai_analysis.root_cause: amf_association_failure detected in NGAP layer with critical severity. This indicates an issue in the NGAP layer functionality, specifically in the association between the gNB and the AMF. In this case, no AMF is associated to the gNB. Common causes include incorrect NGAP message handling, missing or misconfigured AMF parameters, missing routing information or protocol mismatches.
suggested_fixes: 1. Recheck and correct AMF IP, port, and gNB configuration files: openairinterface5g/targets/PROJECTS/GENERIC-NR-5GC/CONF/cu_gnb.conf
2. Validate network configuration and ensure AMF parameters are correctly set in configuration files like AMF IP, port,
3. Check if the AMF is reachable from the gNB
4. Ensure PLMN, TAI, and slices in gNB match those in AMF.
5. Add a static route to the AMF machine if it is running on a different network or interface: ip route add <AMF_IP> via <gNB_IP> dev <interface>
function_analysis: Review error handling and logging in NGAP association setup
Verify AMF parameters and network configuration
Check for protocol mismatches or unsupported NGAP messages
